headlines.title: GENERAL MANAGERS' MEETING THURSDAY
headlines.subtitle: Galveston Trunk Line Rate on Cotton and Farm Products to Be Discussed. COMPROMISE IS EXPECTED. Up-State Railroads Claim New Tariff Unfair-Both Sides Threatening Trouble.
headlines.fullText: General managers of Texas railroads will meet in Fort Worth Thursday. One of the subjects to be discussed is the recent dividend change which the Galveston trunk lines have forced on the up.state railroads on through rates on cotton and farm products through Galveston. In addition to reducing the Inand ram cadre shouldered the Gal- vesten terminal expenses on the inland roads, and as a result the two sides are threatening each other with war to the knife. The general on managers may effect a compromise on the controversy when they meet Thursday and thereby avert court troubles. The Galveston trunk lines, viz: Katy, Santa Fe. Trinity & Brazos Valley, and Sun- set-Central lines have notified the Fort Worth & Denver, Cotton Belt, Rock Is. land, Texas Midland, Marshall & East Texas, Orient and all other interior roads that there will be a radical and sweeping change modmang local farm products use consigned or export. This naiveston change will materially increase the earnings of the trunk lines, while it will seriously de. crease the earnings of the Interior lines. In addition, the trunk lines served notice on the up-state roads that Pan would traffic through Galveston. Velasco and Texas City there shall at first be deducted, as representing terminal ex- penses, the sum of 2 cents on carload lots and 5 cents on less than carload lots, these arbitraring shall accrue to the trunk line hauling the freight to Chuter. The Interior roads have threatened to establish through rates to New Orleans at a parity with the Galveston rates.

headlines.title: AGREEMENT IN LEVEE DISPUTE IS SOUGHT
headlines.subtitle: State Reclamation Engineer Will Confer With Opposing Factions.
headlines.fullText: Arthur A. Stiles, state reclamation engineer, will endeavor to get the etty commission and levee board together In those settlement of The the dispute between bodies. The meeting may be held this afternoon. Wednesday morning Stiles visited the Lake Worth levee. Tuesday he was taken over the The dispute between the city and levee board is of years' standing. The city refuses to let the gaps in the levee above the waterworks plant be closed unless protection is afforded for the waterworks by a counter even stage of water is not due to the eyes but to the building of the Nutt dam and other obstructions the city has permitted to be placed in the river. Several efforts to reach an agree. ment have falled.

headlines.title: BIG GAIN IS SHOWN IN SHEEP RECEIPTS
headlines.subtitle: Hoge Alse Show Increase for Year, Good Weather Keeps Cattle on Ranges, Is Belief.
headlines.fullText: The largest percentage of gain in a market division for the May receipts of the Fort Worth livestock was in the sheep division, according to the month- ly statement of the Fort Worth stock . cards Company. received cents month, while boas for the period from Jan 1 to June 1 showed a gain of about 73,000 head. A noticealite falling off in the receipts of cattle and calves is attributed to been receiving, assuring pasturage for long the summer The total receipts last month were as follows: Cattle, 84.936; calves, 11,- 544; hogs, 35,596; sheep, 88,538; horses and mules, 2,024.

headlines.title: Dynamite 'Hero' Who Plots for Promotion Held
headlines.fullText: Ry Associated Press. WASHINGTON, June 3 .- Daniel V. that he had planted four sticks or namite in the department of agricul- tere building yesterday, that he might discover them and win a promotion for heroism, was held for the grand jury today. He was jailed in default of ball on a charge of attempting to dynamite a building.

headlines.title: ELEVEN GRADUATED FROM NURSE'S SCHOOL
